title: Week 3 Day 4 (2/2) — Inside the transformer
summary: Part 2 of 2 · Embedding · decoder layers · LM head · attention · nonlinearity.

sections.heading: High-level stack (Llama 3.2 ~1B)
sections.remember: **Embedding** → **N decoder layers** → **LM head** — each row in `print(model)` is part of this tree.
points: **Decoder layers:** self-attention + MLP + layer norm (×16 for 3.2 1B; ×32 for larger 3.1).
**LM head:** hidden state → logits over **vocab size** (~128k) = next-token probabilities.

sections.heading: Inside one decoder layer
sections.remember: **Self-attention** (Q, K, V, O) decides what prior context matters; **MLP** blends features with a **nonlinearity** in the gate.
points: Attention: from *Attention Is All You Need* — query/key/value learned weights.

sections.heading: Why nonlinearity matters
sections.remember: Stacked **linear** layers collapse to one linear map — **ReLU/SiLU** in the gate makes deep parameters meaningful.
points: Analogy: many audio mixers in series without distortion = one mixer.
Llama uses **SiLU** (smooth ReLU variant) — choice is somewhat empirical.
You don't need to derive this — intuition for reading `print(model)`.

sections.heading: Next-token prediction
sections.remember: Output is **one distribution over the whole vocab** per forward pass — generation loops: pick token → append → repeat (Day 5).
points: Training improves weights; inference only runs this stack.

visual.caption: One forward pass → probabilities for every possible next token

cheatSheet: Attention = what to focus on · MLP = mix + SiLU
